af_score_components.notes: Has official MCP server (quality 84). Supabase — PostgreSQL backend platform with auto-generated REST API (PostgREST), realtime, auth, and storage. Three credential tiers: anon key (public, respects RLS), service_role key (bypasses ALL RLS — treat as root), Personal Access Token (management API). CRITICAL: Free tier projects pause after 1 week of inactivity — 503 errors on cold start. Schema cache refresh required when new tables are added. GraphQL at separate /graphql/v1 endpoint. Storage file URLs expire. Edge functions run on Deno, not Node.js. Management API requires separate PAT from project API keys. Retry guidance documented.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS mandatory. Three-tier credential model: anon key (safe for client-side, RLS-controlled), service_role key (root-equivalent — bypasses all RLS, never expose to clients), PAT for management. SOC2, GDPR, HIPAA compliant. Row Level Security is the security model — critical to configure before using anon key in agents. service_role key via secure environment variable (treat as database root credential). Strong open-source transparency. Webhook signatures available.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Status page, SOC2/HIPAA. Stable PostgREST auto-generated API. Free tier cold start is a documented reliability issue. Schema cache refresh documented workaround. Retry guidance documented. Offset-based pagination on REST API. Very active development — occasional deprecations. Realtime subscriptions require explicit table enablement. Very reliable for production backends globally.

af_score_components.notes: Official Supabase MCP server (quality 82). Full access to Supabase project — database queries, schema management, auth, storage, edge functions. Auth via Personal Access Token (PAT). CRITICAL: service_role key gives unrestricted database access — agents using it bypass all Row Level Security. Free tier cold start applies here too. Database branch management (multiple environments) requires Pro plan. Storage URLs expire. Edge function deployment requires Deno runtime knowledge. npx dependency for running server. Retry guidance documented.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS mandatory. PAT for MCP server auth. service_role key bypass of RLS is the primary security concern — documented in gotchas. SOC2, GDPR, HIPAA compliant. PAT and service_role keys via environment variables. Official Supabase maintenance ensures timely security updates. Project-scoped access provides isolation between Supabase projects.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Official Supabase maintenance. Status page, SOC2. Free tier cold start documented. Stable database and schema operations. Storage URL expiry requires correct agent handling. npx runtime dependency. Retry guidance documented. Active development with good backward compatibility practices. Very reliable for database management workflows.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. SurveyMonkey survey platform REST API. auth_complexity_score=65 — OAuth2 mandatory, no API key option; adds complexity for unattended agents. Long-lived access tokens (no expiry by default). Free tier 40-response limit per survey. API response data does not include question text — requires separate question lookup by ID. Bulk response retrieval limited to 100 per page. Retry guidance documented. HIPAA compliance requires Enterprise plan. No SDK — use raw HTTP. Rate limits per plan.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS mandatory. OAuth2 with granular scopes (surveys_read, surveys_write, responses_read, responses_write, etc.). SOC2, GDPR, HIPAA (Enterprise plan) compliant. Long-lived tokens reduce refresh complexity. OAuth client credentials via environment variables. No fine-grained per-survey access control. Enterprise HIPAA requires explicit business agreement and plan upgrade.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: SurveyMonkey status page, SOC2. Stable REST API. Retry guidance documented. Bulk response pagination (100/page) requires multiple requests for large surveys. Response data structure requires question ID lookup for full context. Offset-based pagination. Generally reliable as enterprise survey infrastructure.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Swell API-first headless commerce platform. Store ID and secret key pair for server-side; public key for client-side storefront. REST and GraphQL APIs — not all operations available on both. Store ID required in every API call — agents must configure per-store for multi-tenant. Subscription billing uses Swell-specific period enum values. Webhook payloads contain full object data — process idempotently. Test and live modes use separate API keys. Cursor-based pagination. Rate limits not documented. SDKs in Node, Python.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS mandatory. Server-side secret key + client-side public key separation is a good security practice. PCI-DSS and GDPR compliant. No fine-grained per-resource scoping on secret key. Test/live environment isolation. Secret key via environment variables. Smaller platform means less security audit breadth than Shopify.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Status page available. REST and GraphQL APIs with decent coverage. REST/GraphQL feature parity not complete. Retry guidance not documented. Cursor-based pagination. Younger platform — less production track record than Shopify or BigCommerce. Webhook duplicate handling required for idempotency.

af_score_components.notes: Has official MCP server (quality 82). Tailscale zero-config mesh VPN management API. OAuth with fine-grained scopes strongly recommended for agents (devices:read, acls:write, etc.) — excellent least-privilege design. CRITICAL: ACL policy updates replace the entire policy — agents must read existing policy before modifying to avoid lockouts. Auth keys have expiry and device count limits. Device approval may be required before enrolled device can communicate. API operates on tailnet slug. Retry guidance documented. Cursor-based pagination.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS mandatory. OAuth clients with fine-grained scopes (devices:read, devices:write, network:read, acls:write, etc.) — best-in-class least-privilege design for network management. SOC2 and GDPR compliant. ACL policy is critical infrastructure — agents must handle with extreme care. OAuth tokens via environment variables. Tailscale sees your device graph — privacy consideration for sensitive networks. Security product with strong internal practices.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Tailscale status page, SOC2. Stable versioned API. MCP server is official but newer — verify capabilities. ACL replace-semantics require careful agent logic to prevent network disruptions. Retry guidance documented. Cursor-based pagination. Auth key management requires proactive rotation. Very reliable as critical networking infrastructure for millions of devices.

af_score_components.notes: Has official MCP server (quality 85). Tavily — search API purpose-built for AI agents. Single API key. Returns structured content optimized for LLM injection. include_raw_content: true is expensive in tokens — use sparingly. max_results defaults to 5; increasing to 10+ increases token cost significantly. Search topic 'news' for recency. No date range filtering. Rate limits per API key — shared keys in multi-agent systems deplete quickly. Results are time-sensitive — don't cache long. Retry guidance documented. LangChain integration available.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS mandatory. Single API key — no scope granularity. No formal compliance certifications documented. Search results from public web — no sensitive data handling by design. API key via tvly-api-key header or request body. Newer SaaS service without published SOC2. Simple security model appropriate for a search API.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Status page available. Stable API with active development. Results inherently non-idempotent (web changes constantly). Rate limits per API key require management in multi-agent. Retry guidance documented. No pagination needed — results returned in single response. Generally reliable for AI agent search workflows as a purpose-built service.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. TaxJar US sales tax automation — real-time calculations, automated filing, nexus management. Simple Bearer token auth — cleanest of the tax APIs. Sandbox and production use different tokens. Transaction creation required for AutoFile — must push orders, not just calculate. Nexus must be configured in TaxJar dashboard before calculations are accurate. Product tax categories must be set for accurate rates. AutoFile subscription required (separate from API). Monthly API call limits need monitoring. SDKs in Python, Ruby, PHP, JS, Java, .NET, Go. Retry guidance documented.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS mandatory. Simple Bearer token — no fine-grained scope control. SOC2 certified. Sandbox/production token isolation. Tax data includes business financial information — appropriate sensitivity. Token via Authorization Bearer/env vars. Fewer compliance certifications than enterprise tax APIs like Avalara but appropriate for mid-market.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Status page, SOC2. Stable API. Retry guidance documented. Monthly API call quota requires tracking. AutoFile is a separate subscription requiring additional setup. Nexus configuration is a prerequisite — misconfiguration causes incorrect calculations. No pagination on most endpoints. Generally reliable for US sales tax automation.

af_score_components.notes: Has MCP server (quality 60). Java thread dump and heap analysis tool — primarily a GUI/VisualVM plugin with a secondary MCP server mode. auth_complexity_score=100 (no auth needed — local stdio MCP). IMPORTANT: Must be launched with -Djava.awt.headless=true for MCP mode or GUI will attempt to open. JSON thread dump support (Java 21+) is experimental. Requires Java 11+ runtime. MCP is a secondary use case — may have rough edges. No rate limits (local tool). Idempotent analysis. Good for JVM performance debugging in AI workflows.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local stdio MCP — no network exposure. No credentials required. Analysis operates on thread dump files — no sensitive API credentials needed. Thread dump files may contain sensitive stack trace information — local processing is appropriate. Java 11+ dependency. Open source with infrequent updates — dependency audit recommended. Appropriate security model for a local analysis tool.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Local tool — no SLA. MCP server is secondary use case with potential rough edges. headless flag requirement is an operational gotcha. JSON thread dump support experimental for Java 21+. Idempotent analysis — same dump produces same results. Java 11+ requirement. Generally reliable for the specific use case of analyzing thread dump files when properly configured.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Telegram Bot API — free messaging platform for agent notifications and conversational interfaces. Simple bot token from @BotFather. IMPORTANT: Per-chat rate limit of 1 msg/second — cannot fan-out to many individual chats quickly. Webhook requires public HTTPS endpoint — local dev needs ngrok. getUpdates (polling) and setWebhook cannot be used simultaneously. Bot cannot initiate conversation with user who hasn't started the bot. Group chats: Privacy Mode hides most messages by default. Inline keyboard callbacks expire after 24 hours. Bot API file size limit 50MB. No message delivery confirmation. Retry guidance documented.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS mandatory for webhook endpoints. Bot token grants full access to all bot capabilities — no scope control. Leaked tokens allow complete bot impersonation — critical to protect. GDPR compliant. No fine-grained permission model. Bot token never expires (but can be revoked via BotFather). Token via environment variables. Messages not end-to-end encrypted in bots/groups (only Secret Chats are E2E). Simple but insecure-by-design token model.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Telegram status page. Extremely stable Bot API with excellent backward compatibility history. Retry guidance documented. Offset-based pagination for getUpdates. Per-chat rate limits require agent throttling. Webhook requires consistent HTTPS availability. Generally very reliable — used by millions of bots globally as free communication infrastructure.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Terraform Cloud REST API for managing IaC workspaces, runs, state, variables. Simple Bearer token — user, team, or org tokens with different access scopes. IMPORTANT: API uses JSON:API format — different from standard REST JSON, requires careful parsing or JSON:API library. Runs have a queue — agents must poll run status. Workspace must be locked for certain operations. Sensitive variables cannot be read back — write-only. VCS-connected workspaces have different run trigger behavior than API-triggered. State file access requires separate permissions. Retry guidance documented. Cursor-based pagination. Go SDK (go-tfe) available.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS mandatory. User tokens, team tokens, and org tokens provide tiered access control — appropriate least-privilege hierarchy. SOC2 Type II and ISO27001 certified. Sensitive variables are write-only — cannot be read back via API (strong secret protection). Token via Authorization Bearer/env vars. Audit logging available for enterprise. HashiCorp security practices appropriate for IaC infrastructure control.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Terraform Cloud status page, SOC2/ISO27001. Stable API. Retry guidance documented. Run queue requires polling — workspace lock status must be checked. Cursor-based pagination. JSON:API format adds parsing complexity. State file management is critical — agents must not corrupt state. VCS-connected workspace trigger differences are documented. Very reliable as core IaC orchestration infrastructure.
